title: Anchored Ledger Entry
description: The envelope schema for the structural-permanence layer. Wraps any JSON payload (RCR entry, blind_spot_log entry, ConsentRecord, change event, etc.) with hash-chain metadata that allows offline verification of integrity. The payload schema is whatever the payload is; this schema validates the envelope around it. See ledger/README.md for layering.

envelope_version.description: Version of the anchored-entry envelope format. v1 = SHA-256 hash, JSON canonicalization via sorted keys + minimal separators.

entry_id.description: Stable unique identifier for this anchored entry. Suggested: anchor-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of envelope construction.

payload_kind.description: What kind of payload is anchored. Conventional values: 'rcr', 'blind_spot_log', 'consent_record', 'change_event', 'arbitrary'. Used by readers to dispatch to the correct payload-schema validator.

payload.description: The anchored payload. Any JSON-serializable structure. The envelope is agnostic to payload structure; it only commits to its hash.

payload_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the canonicalized payload. Canonicalization = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':'), ensure_ascii=False).

previous_entry_hash.description: entry_hash of the prior chain entry, or null for the genesis entry. Forms the hash chain that detects insertion / reordering / deletion.

entry_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the canonicalized chain-input. Chain-input = {entry_id, timestamp, payload_kind, payload_hash, previous_entry_hash, hash_algorithm, envelope_version} canonicalized as JSON.

hash_algorithm.description: Hash algorithm used. v1 supports sha256 only; future envelope versions may support others.

signatures.description: Optional list of signatures over entry_hash. Backends that support signing (chain-of-custody) populate this; others leave it empty or omit.
